prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: A CPU is like a computer's brain, honey - it does all the thinking while the computer is running. Just like how my brain tells my hands to bake your favorite cookies, the CPU tells all the parts of the computer what jobs to do and when. The better the CPU, the faster your computer can think and respond, which is why some computers feel much zippier than others. It's a tiny little square chip inside the computer, but oh my, it works so hard handling all your games and videos! Without a CPU, your computer would just be a box full of parts that don't know how to talk to each other.
